title: After State-run schools, Vande Mataram now compulsory in all Bengal madrasas
content: “If singing ‘Vande Mataram’ is compulsory in those schools, why should the same not be mandatory in recognised madrasas,” Minister Khudiram Tudu asked

title: T.N. Cabinet expansion updates: 23 more Ministers take oath; Congress joins Vijay-led Cabinet
content: With the latest expansion, a total of 33 Ministers are now part of the TVK-led government in Tamil Nadu, including two from Congress.

title: Andhra Pradesh appoints 25 artistes to Nataka Academy; members meet Minister Durgesh
content: Tourism and Culture Minister Kandula Durgesh felicitates the members and asks them to widen theatre’s reach and build cultural awareness among the youth

title: West Bengal government to detect, delete and deport illegal infiltrators, says CM Adhikari
content: The West Bengal government handed over land for construction of border fencing along 27 km of India Bangladesh border across five districts of the State

title: Major fire at hosiery shop in Hyderabad’s Pathergatti
content: The fire broke out at a complex metres away from Gulzar Houz where a fire on May 18, 2025 killed over a dozen people

title: BJP opens two-day Deendayal Upadhyaya training programme for State leaders
content: The party plans welfare outreach programmes from June 5 to 21; a State leader praises Modi’s global standing and faults the YSRCP on fuel taxes

title: Newly elected MLAs sworn in as 16th Kerala Assembly convenes
content: Pro Tem Speaker G. Sudhakaran administers oath to MLAs-designate over a three-and-a-half-hour-long ceremony

title: Thin line between defamation and criticism: Delhi HC on Raghav Chadha seeking protection of personality rights
content: It referred to precedent in the Shashi Tharoor case where the court protected the personality rights of the Congress leaders because of his distinctive style of speech and demeanour

title: India needs radical reset in economic policy-making; Modi government has run out of ideas: Congress
content: Prevailing atmosphere of fear and business uncertainty in the investment community caused by policy flip-flops, administrative orders, tax notices, raids and threats of raids by tax authorities and investigative agencies, says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh in a statement

title: Marie Wilson in T.N’s new Finance Minister, Sengottaiyan gets Revenue; portfolios allocated for new Ministers of Vijay-led Cabinet
content: Mr. Vijay retained his existing departments and was additionally allotted Special Initiatives, Poverty Alleviation and Rural Indebtedness

title: Around 43% polling in Falta repoll till 11 a.m.; voting peaceful
content: A total of 2.36 lakh electors -- 1,21,300 men, 1,15,135 women and nine third gender individuals -- are eligible to exercise their franchise in the repoll, which started at 7 am and will continue till 6 pm.

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Absconding husband applies for bail in Jabalpur High Court
content: A magistrate court had earlier rejected his anticipatory bail application while granting it to his mother, Giribala Singh, a retired District Sessions Judge.

title: Supreme Court bats for victim-centric approach, refuses to entertain plea for consolidation of FIRs
content: The CJI referred to recent amendments in criminal law and said now the rights of victims have been acknowledged

title: Hyderabad airport posts Ebola advisory for passengers arriving from three countries
content: Health authorities have asked travellers developing symptoms within 21 days of arrival to immediately seek medical care and cooperate with public health screening measures

title: Defeat of delimitation Bill is south India’s loss: Nara Lokesh
content: The vote against Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill has placed southern States at a disadvantage — Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Telangana and Andhra Pradesh stand to lose representation after the next Census, Telugu Desam Party’s newly appointed working president says, blaming politics, not policy for the Bill’s failure; he addresses concerns over upcoming resource-intensive data centres and explains why his government is incentivising larger families

title: 27 Maoists surrender before police in Ranchi
content: The surrender, part of the ongoing ‘Operation Navjeevan’, marks a significant step in the Jharkhand government’s efforts to combat Maoist violence

title: T.N. Cabinet portfolios: CM Vijay to hold Home; Finance for Sengottaiyan; Aadhav Arjuna gets PWD
content: N. Anand has been assigned Rural Development and Water Resources, whereas S. Keerthana has been allocated Industries

title: The flamingo phenomenon: Why photographers and birders flock to Pulicat lake
content: Pulicat, a historic lagoon town near Chennai, draws birders and photographers from across South India who go on boating trips to see the graceful flamingo in still waters

title: Government issues Ebola advisory for passengers arriving from DR Congo, Uganda and South Sudan
content: Union Health Ministry officials clarified that no case of Ebola has been detected in India so far; it advised passengers to watch out for symptoms such as fever, weakness, headache, muscle pain, vomiting, diarrhoea, sore throat and unexplained bleeding

title: Jharkhand family buries wrong body after mistaken identity; missing man returns alive
content: Police have now begun the process of exhuming the body to conduct a DNA test to identify the deceased correctly

title: Congress workers protest over NEET irregularities in Jaipur; police use water cannons to stop march
content: The Congress claimed that the future of nearly 22 lakh students had been affected due to the alleged leak

title: Over 10,000 Kedarnath pilgrims rescued after massive landslip in Uttarakhand’s Rudraprayag
content: SDRF Commandant Arpan Yaduvanshi said the teams stationed along the pilgrimage route maintained high alertness.

title: U.S. shuts India-based call centre operation that defrauded elderly Americans
content: U.S. authorities said tech support scams cost Americans $2.1 billion last year, while residents of Rhode Island alone reported losses of at least $5.7 million

title: CM Vijay-led Cabinet in Tamil Nadu expands to 33; Congress joins government after 59 years
content: Mr. Vijay’s Cabinet is likely to undergo one more round of expansion, as the TVK has extended an open invitation to the VCK and IUML, both of which contested as part of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance (SPA) and have two MLAs each

title: Palnadu Collector suspends surveyor, VRO over alleged bribery in land resurvey
content: Villagers tell a review meeting the two officials had allegedly demanded bribes for land measurements and revenue records; the Collector warns of stern action

title: TVK-led Tamil Nadu government: full list of Ministers
content: The specific portfolios for the nine Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) ministers were formally announced on Saturday (May 16, 2026), with Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay retaining key departments, including the Home and Police.

title: Bengaluru delivery executive arrested for committing unnatural act with cow
content: The incident came to light after CCTV footage of the act went viral on social media, triggering public outrage and demands for strict action against the accused.

title: Bidadi township: 80% landowners agree to part with land, says DKS
content: The government is set to acquire over 6,700 acres for developing India’s first and largest artificial intelligence (AI)-powered integrated township

title: World Museum Day: Exploring Visakhapatnam’s museums beyond history
content: From decommissioned war machines and Buddhist relics to butterfly trails, Visakhapatnam’s museums are broadening the meaning of what a museum can hold

title: Injectable cosmetic products not permitted under law, says drug regulator CDSCO
content: “Products supplied in form of injectable preparation do not fall under the definition of cosmetics. No cosmetic is permitted to be used as injection by consumer/professionals/ aesthetic clinics,” the notice said.

title: SAEL to invest ₹1,200 crore in 300 MW solar plant in Kadapa; Lokesh to open unit on May 22
content: The company has already put ₹830.40 crore into a 207.60-MW plant, part of a 600-MW build expected to cut 21.6 million tonnes of CO2 emissions over 25 years

title: Will BJP leaders take public transport daily, or just for photoshoot: D.K. Shivakumar
content: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar accuses BJP leaders of using public transport only for photoshoots

title: Synthetic variants, vanishing trees threaten legacy of the Nilgiris eucalyptus oil
content: An application seeking a Geographical Indication (GI) tag for the Nilgiris Eucalyptus Thailam has been filed, but some manufacturers express doubt over whether the move can revive the industry

title: Andhra Pradesh intensifies fertiliser crackdown; 80 dealer licences suspended, one cancelled
content: The licence of dealers found guilty will be cancelled immediately and criminal cases registered against them, says Atchannaidu

title: Andhra Pradesh races to finish 228 PM-KUSUM solar plants by December, says CS K. Vijayanand
content: He stresses need for weekly monitoring, faster approvals, and stronger coordination between DISCOMs and developers

title: Which party governs which State/UT: In map
content: Results of Assembly elections across twelve States and two UTs since January 2024 has marked a shift in the political landscape. Here is a look at the change in ruling governments in maps.

title: PM Modi returns to India after five-nation tour
content: The prime minister embarked on his tour on May 15, visiting the United Arab Emirates, the Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, and Italy over a course of several days

title: India calls out Pakistan’s ‘long-tainted record of genocidal acts’ at UNSC
content: Harish Parvathaneni's remarks came after Pakistan’s representative raised the issue of Jammu and Kashmir at the debate.

title: Kerala Cabinet 2026: Full list of Ministers
content: The detailed portfolios will be submitted to the Governor and will be published via gazette notification

title: Jagan pays tributes to Prakasam Pantulu, Kasu Brahmananda Reddy and Sirivennela
content: A.P. Brahmin Association delegation seeks a memorial park for Prakasam Pantulu in Amaravati and his statue on the Assembly premises

title: Stray dog euthanasia: Bengaluru civic bodies examining legally permissible options for ‘rabid, incurably ill or demonstrably dangerous or aggressive dogs’
content: The Supreme Court underscored that the authorities concerned must undertake euthanasia only after due assessment by qualified veterinary experts and strictly in accordance with applicable statutory protocols

title: After High Court verdict, Hindu petitioners seek free entry for devotees at Bhojshala in Madhya Pradesh
content: Petitioner Ashish Goyal, associated with the 'Hindu Front for Justice', in his application to the ASI on Wednesday (May 20, 2026), said worshippers should not be charged even the existing Re 1 entry fee

title: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah holds closed-door meeting on SIR; issue to come up before Cabinet
content: This will be the first time that the Congress government in Karnataka is formally discussing the SIR and its implications at the Cabinet level

title: Kannur University launches accident insurance cover for students
content: A total of 17,343 students from Kannur, Kasaragod and Wayanad districts enrolled from 2025 onwards will be covered under the scheme

title: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah instructs School Education Dept. to start tender process to open 800 KPS
content: Around 1,200 children will receive admission in each KPS, and education will be provided in Kannada and English medium from LKG to II Pre-University Course (PUC)

title: Eviction drive at Malayidomthuruth in Kerala: Police register case against over 50 persons
content: Case registered based on a complaint filed by senior lawyer Jayapalan M.K, who led Advocate Commission that tried to carry out the eviction drive

title: Supreme Court ‘inclined’ to grant bail to 2020 Delhi riots accused Tasleem Ahmed and Abdul Khalid Saifi
content: Court says it is prima facie inclined to grant bail to 2020 Delhi riots accused Tasleem Ahmed and Abdul Khalid Saifi; ASG argues that conflicting views on bail in UAPA cases need to be settled by a larger Bench

title: MANN+HUMMEL opens tech, innovation centre in Karnataka
content: The new facility is designed to accelerate global product development and customer-centric innovation by integrating advanced research labs, testing infrastructure, digital engineering, and data analytics under one roof.

title: Congress has pushed Karnataka from revenue surplus to deficit, alleges Capt. Brijesh Chowta
content: Only achievement of Siddaramaiah government during past three years is to push progressive Karnataka to the debt trap, says MP

title: U.P. CM Adityanath conducts review of Data Centre Cluster, Project Ganga and wheat processing policy
content: The Chief Minister said that the data centre cluster will create the basic infrastructure for Uttar Pradesh’s AI mission

title: Supreme Court suspects Karnataka government of ‘delaying tactics’ but extends Bengaluru civic polls’ deadline to August 31
content: The apex court said this would be the State government’s ‘last chance’, and there would be no further extensions

title: Kerala Assembly: MLA-designates arrive for swearing-in on foot, bicycle, vehicles
content: Congress MLA-elect from Puthuppally Chandy Oommen, son of former Kerala CM Oommen Chandy, arrived on a bicycle wearing a shirt with an image of his father on it

title: 'Promiscuity can be a habit': New audio captures exchange between Twisha’s brother, mother-in-law
content: A leaked audio recording allegedly features retired judge Giri Bala Singh questioning Twisha Sharma about her past sexual history and fidelity.  Meanwhile, police have increased the reward for information leading to the arrest of Twisha's husband, Samarth Singh, who is wanted in a dowry harassment case.

title: 'Don't do BJP politics': DMK fumes as Vande Mataram played first at Vijay govt's oath event again
content: A controversy erupted in Tamil Nadu over the recitation order of national and state songs during a swearing-in ceremony. The DMK criticized the Vijay government for prioritizing 'Vande Mataram' over the Tamil invocation song, accusing them of disrespecting Tamil culture and adopting BJP's politics.

title: Rs 600 crore rail land, Eid fears: Why Mumbai eviction drive turned chaotic
content: Western Railway commenced a massive anti-encroachment drive at Garib Nagar, Mumbai, demolishing hundreds of structures near Bandra station to reclaim valuable railway land. The operation, aimed at facilitating railway expansion, faced resistance and violence, resulting in injuries and detentions. Residents allege inadequate rehabilitation and express distress over demolitions preceding Eid.

title: 'We could have handled it better': Tamim Iqbal on Bangladesh’s T20 WC pull out
content: Former Bangladesh captain Tamim Iqbal opens up on his transition into cricket administration after a heart attack, Bangladesh’s fast bowling rise, the future of India-Bangladesh relations, the growing influence of T20 leagues. Bangladesh Cricket Board's interim president also shares his vision to restore Bangladesh cricket’s reputation, which has taken a beating after they decided to pull out from the T20 World Cup.

title: Muga silk stole for Meloni, mangoes for UAE Prez: What PM Modi gifted leaders during 5-nation tour
content: Prime Minister Modi's recent five-nation tour saw him present world leaders with culturally rich Indian gifts. From Assam's golden Muga silk to Manipur's Shirui Lily stoles and Agra's marble inlay boxes, these presents showcased India's diverse traditional crafts, agricultural bounty, and musical heritage, fostering cultural exchange.

title: HSBC CEO Georges Elhedery to employees: AI will destroy some jobs, stop fighting with us
content: HSBC CEO Georges Elhedery urged employees to embrace AI, acknowledging it will eliminate some roles while creating new ones. This comes as the bank reportedly explores a significant overhaul potentially impacting 20,000 jobs globally. HSBC is investing in AI tools and staff training to navigate this automation shift.

title: 'Amra Korbo Joy': Bangladesh celebrate Pakistan Test sweep with special song - Watch
content: Bangladesh achieved a historic 2-0 Test series whitewash against Pakistan, culminating in an emotional dressing room celebration. Players united to sing "Amra Korbo Joy" after their dominant 78-run victory in the second Test. This triumph signifies Bangladesh's growing confidence and consistency in Test cricket.

title: 42 aircraft, over $29 billion: What Trump’s Iran operation cost the US military
content: Operation Epic Fury, initiated under the leadership of Donald Trump, saw the U.S. military suffer grave losses, with 42 aircraft—comprising fighter jets and drones—sustaining damage or being lost entirely. This aggressive strike prompted swift and vigorous reprisal from Iran, bringing the region to a boiling point.

title: Gold, silver outlook: Will gold head to Rs 1.70 lakh/10 grams & silver hit Rs 3 lakh/kg?
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: While MCX Silver is consolidating on the weekly chart, gold is eyeing its next meaningful target of Rs 1.7 lakh, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: TCS clarifies gratuity exclusion, says employees will see no reduction in pay
content: TCS employees are reacting to appraisal letters, with some reporting lower take-home salaries due to changes in compensation structure and the exclusion of gratuity from CTC. The IT giant denies any reduction in gross or net pay, stating the revised framework aligns with new labour codes and aims to protect employee earnings while enhancing tax efficiency.

title: NEET UG 2026 exam fee refund process explained: Key NTA rules for students
content: The NTA is set to roll out a refund portal this Thursday for students retaking NEET UG 2026, providing refunds that vary by category, though gateway fees are excluded. Re-examinations will occur on June 21 without any added costs.

title: ‘Neck pain, dizziness ...’: Watson reveals worrying injury update on Raghuvanshi
content: Kolkata Knight Riders secured a crucial win against Mumbai Indians, but their victory was overshadowed by a worrying injury to star batter Angkrish Raghuvanshi. Colliding with a teammate while attempting a catch, Raghuvanshi suffered neck pain, dizziness, and headaches, forcing him out of the match. The franchise is concerned about the impact on their playoff push.

title: UPSC calendar 2027: Civil Services prelims on May 23; check complete schedule here
content: The Union Public Service Commission has unveiled its 2027 exam calendar, featuring key dates for major recruitment drives. The Civil Services Preliminary Examination is scheduled for May 23, 2027, with the Mains commencing on August 20, 2027. Aspirants can find notification release dates, application deadlines, and examination durations for numerous other crucial tests on the official UPSC website.

title: SpaceX hiring world-class engineers, physicists; Musk to personally review applications
content: SpaceX is aggressively seeking top engineering and physics talent for its new SpaceXAI division, with Elon Musk personally reviewing applications. This recruitment drive follows SpaceX's filing for a massive IPO, revealing ambitious plans in AI infrastructure and orbital computing.

title: Why did a woman who stabbed her husband walk free with just a Rs 500 fine?
content: The Telangana High Court upheld a woman's conviction for stabbing her husband to death during a heated argument but reduced her sentence to a Rs 500 fine. The court applied a legal exception for homicides occurring in a sudden fight without premeditation. While her knowledge of causing death was established, the circumstances led to a lenient sentencing, avoiding jail time.

title: Ronda Rousey’s net worth 2026: Earnings, endorsements and lifestyle
content: Ronda Rousey, a trailblazer in combat sports, is projected to have a net worth of nearly $14 million by 2026. Her fortune stems from lucrative UFC and WWE careers, acting, endorsements, and book sales. Rousey's impact on women's MMA and her transition to WWE cemented her status as a crossover star, ensuring her continued relevance and earning potential.

title: Bryson DeChambeau slammed after moon landing claims, PGA flop
content: Bryson DeChambeau went viral after questioning the Apollo 11 moon landing footage during a podcast appearance. The comments came after he missed the cut at the PGA Championship. Fans heavily criticized his opinion online, while DeChambeau also opened up about possibly choosing YouTube content creation over professional golf in the future.

title: Jeff Bezos favored to buy Seahawks from Microsoft co-founder’s estate
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos is projected as the leading buyer for the Seattle Seahawks. The NFL team was owned by late former Microsoft co-founder Paul Allen. After his passing in 2018, it is now managed by her sister Jody Allen under the Paul G. Allen Trust. It is now officially up for sale. Jeff Bezos has shown interest in owning a local NFL franchise for over seven years. The sale process is expected to conclude by the 2026 offseason.

title: 'I grew up watching CSK play': Sai Sudharsan shares Chennai connection
content: Gujarat Titans opener Sai Sudharsan shared his deep emotional ties to Chennai Super Kings ahead of their IPL 2026 clash.  He fondly recalled idolizing CSK during his childhood, making his maiden IPL century against them in 2024 particularly special. Sudharsan, a consistent performer, continues his impressive run this season.

title: PM Modi’s five-nation tour secures nearly $40bn investment pipeline for India
content: Prime Minister Modi's five-nation tour secured nearly $40 billion in investment, with over 50 global firms pledging fresh capital and expansion. Key sectors like semiconductors, defence, and energy saw significant commitments.  India elevated ties with the UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, and Italy, fostering strategic partnerships and boosting economic cooperation.

title: Tamil Nadu cabinet gets bigger under Vijay; Congress returns after nearly six decades
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay is set to expand his cabinet, inducting 23 MLAs, including two from the Congress party, marking their return after 60 years. The Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi (VCK) and IUML are also expected to join the council of ministers soon.

title: Noida’s ‘carbage’ nightmare: Parks and police compounds buried under mountains of abandoned vehicles
content: Hundreds of vehicles, seized in legal battles, are accumulating in Noida's police station parking lots and public spaces, turning them into scrap junkyards. These vehicles, often in working condition when seized, deteriorate due to exposure, leading to financial losses and environmental concerns. Delays in court permissions and administrative processes hinder timely auctions, exacerbating the congestion.

title: 'Report, seek medical care': DGHS issues advisory for flyers from Ebola-hit countries
content: India has intensified Ebola preparedness following outbreaks in Uganda and DRC. The Directorate General of Health Services issued advisories for travelers, urging immediate reporting of symptoms like fever or bleeding within 21 days of arrival. States are enhancing surveillance, screening, and hospital readiness, though no cases have been detected domestically.

title: Epic Fury 2.0? Tracking US military buildup as Trump threatens to end Iran ceasefire
content: America's Operation Epic Fury against Iran has significantly strained military readiness, deploying a vast array of naval, air, and missile-defense assets.  With two of three aircraft carriers at sea and a third of Arleigh Burke destroyers committed, the US faces long-term readiness concerns.  This extensive commitment, while projecting overwhelming force, highlights the risks of strategic overextension.

title: Fresh trouble for Hardik Pandya! MI skipper fined for code of conduct breach
content: Mumbai Indians skipper Hardik Pandya has been fined and received a demerit point for breaching the IPL Code of Conduct. The incident occurred during MI's defeat to Kolkata Knight Riders when Pandya forcefully knocked the bails off the stumps. This disciplinary issue adds to Mumbai's disappointing season, as they were already eliminated from playoff contention.

title: After drubbing in Bihar, Prashant Kishor opts for ashram life till next polls
content: Prashant Kishor has relocated to a Patna ashram until the Bihar assembly polls, aiming for his Jan Suraaj Party to make an impact after a previous electoral setback. He criticized Chief Minister Nitish Kumar's son's cabinet induction and urged voters to prioritize their future over caste or monetary inducements, warning of potential economic challenges from West Asian conflicts.

title: Gunfire outside new Cricket Canada president’s house amid corruption scandal
content: Canadian cricket faces a deepening crisis after shots were fired at the home of its new president, Arvinder Khosa, in what police believe is an extortion-linked attack. This follows recent revelations of alleged organized crime links, player pressure, and spot-fixing concerns during the T20 World Cup. The sport's governing body has also frozen Cricket Canada's funding due to integrity issues.

title: Op Octopus cracks pan-India ‘ghost SIM’ mafia: 66 held across 13 states
content: Hyderabad police, in a massive crackdown across 13 states, have apprehended 66 individuals supplying ghost SIM cards to cybercriminals.  Operation Octopus has successfully targeted the illegal issuance of these SIMs, which were instrumental in 76 fraud cases, resulting in a staggering Rs 101 crore loss.

title: 24K love for gold: Why is India obsessed with the yellow metal?
content: India's enduring love for gold spans centuries, evolving from tradition to modern investment.  Women hold vast reserves, and the market is set for significant growth.  Digital gold and ETFs are gaining traction alongside traditional jewellery.  Gold remains a symbol of prosperity, security, and auspiciousness for Indians across generations.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Top stock market recommendations: Aakash K Hindocha, Deputy Vice President - WM Research, Nuvama Professional Clients Group has recommended Varun Beverages, Max Healthcare Institute, and Page Industries as the top buy calls for May 21, 2026. He also shares his outlook on Nifty, and Bank Nifty.

title: Delhi double murder: Woman, teen son killed; cash, jewellery stolen in robbery
content: A shocking double murder has occurred in Govindpuri, Delhi. A 38-year-old woman, Sharda Sahu, and her 13-year-old son were found stabbed to death. The incident took place in the early hours of Thursday. Police suspect a robbery as cash and jewellery are missing. Investigations are underway to apprehend the culprits.

title: IPL 2026 scripts history! KKR’s win over Mumbai Indians sets an all-time record
content: IPL 2026 has set a new record with 42 successful chases, surpassing the 2016 season's mark. Kolkata Knight Riders' victory over Mumbai Indians cemented this achievement, highlighting a significant trend favoring teams batting second. This shift has sparked discussions about potential disadvantages for first-batting sides in the league.

title: 'Papa, I will fully shoulder...': Rahul remembers father Rajiv with emotional note
content: Rahul Gandhi paid tribute to his father, former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi, on his 35th death anniversary, vowing to fulfill his vision for a strong and prosperous India. Senior Congress leaders also paid homage at Vir Bhumi, remembering Rajiv Gandhi's contributions to modernizing India through technological and educational reforms.

title: Rupee rebounds 61 paise from record low, rises to 96.25 against US dollar
content: The Indian Rupee saw a welcome rebound, gaining 61 paise to trade at 96.25 against the US dollar, driven by easing oil prices. Despite this recovery, the currency remains under pressure, marking a nine-session losing streak. The Reserve Bank of India announced a $5 billion dollar-rupee swap auction to bolster banking system liquidity amidst global uncertainties and foreign investor outflows.

title: Vande Mataram now compulsory in Bengal madrassas: Suvendu govt passes new order
content: West Bengal's school education department has mandated the singing of 'Vande Mataram' during morning assemblies in state-run schools and madrassas, effective immediately. This directive follows a Union home ministry call to mark 150 years of the national song. Schools are seeking clarification on whether the state song will continue alongside 'Vande Mataram' and the National Anthem.

title: BCCI worried whether Rohit Sharma can stay fit till 2027 ODI World Cup
content: As the ODI World Cup approaches, concerns are rising regarding Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya's physical condition. Despite their inclusion in the Afghanistan series, the BCCI is wary of their effectiveness in the 50-over game. The selectors are urgently seeking information on Rohit's overall fitness and Hardik's bowling readiness, emphasizing the necessity for backup opening batsmen.

title: Man charged with stealing from Bondi shooting victim
content: Australian police said Thursday they had charged a man with allegedly stealing from a victim during last year's deadly mass...

title: Tamil Anthem Position Sparks Fresh Outrage At TVK Government's Oath Event
content: The Tamil anthem was again played last as 23 MLAs took oath as ministers in the Vijay-led coalition government on Thursday.

title: Goldman Sach CEO Slides Into Musk's DMs During Bid To Lead SpaceX IPO
content: Banks have been battling for months to take the top spot on the initial public offering for SpaceX, which is targeting a valuation of more than $2 trillion while raising $75 billion.

title: Twisha Sharma's Body Needs -80 Degrees Facility. It Doesn't Exist In Bhopal
content: The court noted that Twisha's body is currently kept in the mortuary at AIIMS Bhopal at -4 degrees Celsius - way higher than it is needed to preserve a body for a longer duration.

title: Sergio Gor Hints At "Big Things" In India-US Nuclear Collaboration
content: The US delegation noted that the recent enactment of the SHANTI Act, 2025, has opened up numerous opportunities for the commercial nuclear industry to work with India. Speaking

title: Ignorant Or Intelligent? Melody Mania Lifts Wrong Parle Stocks, Again
content: PM Modi-Meloni Meet: Melody is still playing on Dalal Street. Only this time, the stock market may not know the lyrics.

title: "I'm Dying": New York Woman Parks Mercedes Next To Manhole, Falls Inside
content: Donike Gocaj, 56, parked her Mercedes-Benz SUV right next to the maintenance hole Monday night and fell in after exiting the vehicle shortly before 11:30 pm.

title: US Seeks Energy Export Boost To India Hit By Hormuz Blockade
content: India is a major energy importer. Before the US-Iran war, India was heavily reliant on Hormuz for supplies of about 90% LPG.

title: SpaceX, The Sprawling Company Targeting The Stars, Mars And An IPO
content: SpaceX is under contract with NASA to produce a modified variant of Starship that would serve as a human landing system.

title: Rocket, Satellite, AI Giant SpaceX Files IPO, Reveals $4.28 Billion Loss
content: SpaceX had a net loss of $4.28 billion on revenue of $4.69 billion for the first quarter, compared with a net loss of $528 million on revenue of about $4 billion a year earlier, the filing shows.

title: The Bail Question: Conflicting Court Views On Umar Khalid Bring UAPA Under Lens Again
content: The Supreme Court on Monday reaffirmed that "bail is the rule and jail the exception" even in cases under the stringent Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA), while expressing serious reservations about its recent verdict denying bail to former

title: Twisha Sharma's Mother-In-Law Faces Dowry Death Case
content: The Madhya Pradesh government has formally initiated proceedings against Giribala Singh after an FIR in the alleged dowry death case was registered against her.

title: A Timeline Of US-Cuba Relations Amid Heightened Tensions In Trump's Second Term
content: Trump has been escalating talk on regime change in Cuba after the military action in Venezuela early this year resulted in the capture of President Nicolas Maduro.

title: "No Faith, No Law, No Morality": India Slams Pak's 'Genocidal Acts' At UNSC
content: The Indian envoy said that such heinous acts of aggression by Pakistan should not come as a surprise from a country that "bombs its own people and conducts systematic genocide".

title: Meet Mbah Sadiman: Man who sold his goats to transform barren hills into forest
content: villagers in a drought-prone region of central Java believed Mbah Sadiman was wasting his life . the elderly farmer spent decades planting banyan trees across barren slopes . he even sold his goats and used much of his own savings, despite being mocked by neighbours who considered the effort pointless .

title: From 'Melodi' camaraderie to strategic deals: Key takeaways from PM Modi’s Italy visit
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
